title: Tom and Jerry
author: William Hanna & Joseph Barbera
content: A classic slapstick cartoon following the endless rivalry between Tom the cat and Jerry the mouse. Known for its fast-paced physical comedy, creative chase sequences, and near-total absence of dialogue.

title: Looney Tunes
author: Various (Warner Bros.)
content: An anthology of animated short films featuring iconic characters like Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Elmer Fudd, and Tweety. Celebrated for sharp wit, fourth-wall breaks, and timeless comedic timing.

title: The Flintstones
author: William Hanna & Joseph Barbera
content: A prehistoric parody of modern suburban life following Fred Flintstone, his wife Wilma, and neighbors Barney and Betty Rubble in the Stone Age town of Bedrock.

title: The Jetsons
author: William Hanna & Joseph Barbera
content: A futuristic sitcom set in 2062 following the Jetson family as they navigate flying cars, robot maids, and space-age gadgets in Orbit City.

title: Scooby-Doo Where Are You!
author: William Hanna & Joseph Barbera
content: Mystery-solving teens Fred, Daphne, Velma, and Shaggy — along with their talking Great Dane Scooby-Doo — travel in the Mystery Machine investigating supposedly supernatural events.

title: The Simpsons
author: Matt Groening
content: A satirical depiction of working-class American life through the dysfunctional Simpson family in Springfield. Homer, Marge, Bart, Lisa, and Maggie have become cultural icons over 30+ seasons.

title: Bugs Bunny Show
author: Chuck Jones & Friz Freleng
content: A cunning and cool-headed rabbit who constantly outsmarts hunters, monsters, and villains with humor and quick thinking. Bugs Bunny is one of the most recognizable cartoon characters in history.

title: Popeye the Sailor
author: Max Fleischer
content: A spinach-powered sailor who gains superhuman strength to rescue his girlfriend Olive Oyl from the villainous Bluto. One of the earliest and most enduring animated icons.

title: DuckTales
author: Jymn Magon
content: Scrooge McDuck and his nephews Huey, Dewey, and Louie embark on treasure-hunting adventures around the world. A beloved Disney afternoon series known for its adventurous spirit and catchy theme song.

title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
author: David Wise
content: Four pizza-loving turtles mutated into humanoid ninjas — Leonardo, Donatello, Raphael, and Michelangelo — fight crime in New York City under the guidance of their rat sensei Splinter.

title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe
author: Michael Halperin
content: Prince Adam transforms into the mighty He-Man to defend Eternia and Castle Grayskull from the evil Skeletor. One of the most iconic fantasy action cartoons of the 1980s.

title: ThunderCats
author: Leonard Starr
content: A group of humanoid cat warriors from the planet Thundera flee their dying world and settle on Third Earth, where their leader Lion-O wields the magical Sword of Omens against the evil Mumm-Ra.

title: Animaniacs
author: Tom Ruegger
content: Yakko, Wakko, and Dot Warner — along with Pinky and the Brain, Slappy Squirrel, and other characters — deliver rapid-fire humor, pop-culture parodies, and educational content.

title: Dexter's Laboratory
author: Genndy Tartakovsky
content: Boy genius Dexter maintains a secret hi-tech laboratory hidden behind his bookshelf, constantly battling his ditzy older sister Dee Dee who keeps stumbling in and causing chaos.

title: The Powerpuff Girls
author: Craig McCracken
content: Three kindergarteners — Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup — created accidentally from sugar, spice, and Chemical X, fight crime and villains in Townsville as superheroes.

title: Johnny Bravo
author: Van Partible
content: A muscle-bound, self-absorbed pretty boy named Johnny Bravo constantly fails at impressing women while stumbling into absurd adventures. A parody of macho pop-culture archetypes.

title: Cow and Chicken
author: David Feiss
content: Cow and Chicken are improbable siblings living with their human parents — who only appear from the waist down. The Red Guy serves as a recurring villain in increasingly bizarre scenarios.

title: Rugrats
author: Arlene Klasky & Gábor Csupó
content: A group of toddlers — Tommy, Chuckie, Phil, Lil, and antagonist Angelica — interpret the adult world around them through their vivid imaginations and childhood logic.

title: Hey Arnold!
author: Craig Bartlett
content: Football-headed fourth-grader Arnold navigates city life in a diverse urban neighborhood with his friends, guided by his wise grandparents and an innate sense of empathy.

title: SpongeBob SquarePants
author: Stephen Hillenburg
content: An enthusiastic and optimistic sea sponge named SpongeBob lives in an underwater pineapple in Bikini Bottom with his best friend Patrick Star, working as a fry cook at the Krusty Krab.
